debug_info.goal:
m : Type u → Type v
inst✝¹ : Monad m
inst✝ : HasEvalSPMF m
α : Type u
mx : m α
x : α
⊢ Pr[=some x | some <$> mx] = Pr[=x | mx]

failed_attempts:
simp_all [distrib_mul_sum, mul_assoc, mul_comm, mul_left_comm]
  <;>
  congr 1 <;> ext <;> simp_all [Option.some.injEq]
  <;>
  aesop

have h₂ : Pr[= some x | some <$> mx] = Pr[= x | mx] := by
    simp_all [HasEvalSPMF.bind, Option.bind, Option.isWhen]
    <;>
    (try simp_all [HasEvalSPMF.bind, Option.bind, Option.isWhen]) <;>
    (try aesop) <;>
    (try simp_all [eval_eq, eval_lt, eval_le, eval_add, eval_mul, eval_one, eval_zero]) <;>
    (try norm_num) <;>
    (try linarith) <;>
    (try ring_nf) <;>
    (try field_simp)
    <;>
    simp_all [eval_eq, eval_lt, eval_le, eval_add, eval_mul, eval_one, eval_zero]
    <;>
    aesop
  exact h₂

-- Use the property of `Pr[= ...]` to directly compare the probabilities
    -- by leveraging the injectivity of `some` and the definition of `evalDist`.
    simp [Pr, evalDist_map, Option.map, Some.injEq]
    <;>
    simp_all [HasEvalSPMF.evalDist]
    <;>
    rfl
  exact h

have h₁ : Pr[= some x | some <$> mx] = evalDist (some <$> mx) (some x) := by
      simp [Pr_eq]
    have h₂ : Pr[= x | mx] = evalDist mx x := by simp [Pr_eq]
    rw [h₁, h₂]
    -- Use the fact that the evaluations are the same because of the injectivity of `some`
    have h₃ : evalDist (some <$> mx) (some x) = evalDist mx x := by
      -- Use the property of evalDist for mapped monads
      simp [evalDist_map]
      <;>
      simp_all [dist_map, Option.map]
      <;>
      simp_all [Dist.bind_apply, Dist.map_apply]
      <;>
      simp_all [dist_map, Option.map]
      <;>
      rfl
    rw [h₃]
  exact h_main

<;> ext <;> simp_all [Option.map, Option.bind, Option.get]
  <;> congr 1 <;> ext <;> simp_all [Option.map, Option.bind, Option.get]
  <;> aesop

have h₂ : Pr[= x | mx] = evalDist x (OptionLikify mx) := rfl
  have h₃ : evalDist (some x) (OptionLikify (some <$> mx)) = evalDist x (OptionLikify mx) := by
    simp [OptionLikify, evalDist]
    <;>
    simp_all [Option.bind_eq, Option.get]
    <;>
    aesop
  rw [h₁, h₂, h₃]

rw [show (some x : Option α) = (some x : Option α) by rfl]
    -- Simplify the expression using the definition of `Pr[= a | mx]` and properties of `some` and `fmap`
    simp [probSome, map_fmap, Option.some_inj]
    <;>
    simp_all [Option.some_inj]
    <;>
    aesop
  -- The final result follows directly from the established equality
  exact h₁

some_inj, Pr, HasEvalSPMF.evalS, eval_map, eval_eq]
    <;>
    simp_all [Option.bind_eq]
    <;>
    congr 1 <;>
    simp [Option.bind]
    <;>
    ext a <;>
    simp_all [Option.bind]
    <;>
    aesop
  
  exact h1

<;>
  (try simp_all [HasEvalSPMF.evalSPMF, HasEvalDist.evalDist]) <;>
  (try congr 1 <;> ext x <;> simp [Function.funext_iff]) <;>
  (try simp_all [Option.some_inj]) <;>
  (try congr 1 <;> ext x <;> simp [Function.funext_iff, Option.some_inj]) <;>
  (try simp_all [Option.some_inj]) <;>
  (try rfl)

simp [map_eq_map, Option.bind_eq_some_iff]
  <;>
  simp_all [Pr.prDescr, HasEvalSPMF.evalDist_eq, eval_map, filter_eq']
  <;>
  congr 1
  <;>
  ext1 val
  <;>
  simp_all [Option.bind_eq_some_iff]
  <;>
  aesop
